title: NIS2 Proportional Security Measure Verification by Tier
exclusions: Does not define what constitutes proportionate security measures per Art.21(2), interpret proportionality under the Implementing Regulation, or replace risk-management judgement. Only compares implemented controls against a pre-defined tier-appropriate baseline.
evidence: `UC-22.2.32` CSV listing `control_id`, `entity_tier`, `search_disabled`, REST capture timestamp, and risk-committee minutes cross-reference stored in `audit_evidence`.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.21(2) is assigned contributing assurance because this UC provides concrete Splunk evidence for the observable control condition, while policy approval, legal interpretation, and official filings remain outside Splunk.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.32: NIS2 Proportional Security Measure Verification by Tier.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.32' running on nis2_control_matrix.csv (control_id, required_for_tier), ES correlation search inventory, nis2_entity_classification.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Disable a non-production ES correlation search whose `title` matches `nis2_<control_id>` for a row where `required_for_tier=true`; the alert must emit that `control_id` with `search_disabled=1`.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Re-enable the same saved search (or delete the test object) and rerun within **15 minutes** so REST returns `disabled=0`, clearing the control from the failure roster.

dataSources: `lookup=nis2_control_matrix.csv` defines `control_id`, `required_for_tier`, `owner_team`. `lookup=nis2_entity_classification.csv` supplies `legal_entity_id`, `entity_tier` for proportionality context. Live enforcement checks pull disabled flags from Splunk Enterprise Security correlation searches via REST (`/servicesNS/-/SplunkEnterpriseSecurity/saved/searches`, Splunkbase 263). Supplement with `index=grc` `sourcetype=grc:risk` to show whether missing controls align to accepted risk treatment.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) optional for control ownership tickets
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_control_matrix.csv
| join entity_tier [
    | inputlookup nis2_entity_classification.csv
    | stats values(entity_tier) as entity_tiers by legal_entity_id
    | mvexpand entity_tiers
    | rename entity_tiers as entity_tier
]
| where required_for_tier="true"
| join control_id [
    | rest /servicesNS/-/SplunkEnterpriseSecurity/saved/searches count=0 splunk_server=local
    | search title="nis2_*"
    | eval control_id=replace(title,"nis2_","")
    | stats latest(disabled) as search_disabled by control_id
]
| where search_disabled=1 OR isnull(search_disabled)
| table control_id, entity_tier, search_disabled
description: Cross-checks the NIS2 control matrix against your active Enterprise Security correlation searches so you can prove required detective controls stay enabled for each entity tier—or expose intentional gaps with a ticket trail.
value: Helps proportionality reviews by translating "which tier needs which control" into live Splunk configuration state instead of slide-deck assertions, so internal audit can trace a disabled search back to a named `control_id`.
implementation: (1) Prefix every mandated correlation search with `nis2_` + `control_id`. (2) Version `nis2_control_matrix.csv` whenever risk committee moves a control between tiers. (3) Save `UC-22.2.32` weekly. (4) Auto-assign SNOW tasks using Splunkbase 1928 alert action. (5) Mirror outputs into GRC workbook quarterly.
visualization: Matrix heatmap of tier vs control coverage, table of disabled required controls, single value for percentage enabled.
knownFalsePositives:
• **ES maintenance bundle disables searches temporarily** — Distinguish: rollout window **30 minutes**. Suppress: join `_internal` `deployment` events or time-bound `nis2_exception_register.csv` with `change_ticket`.
• **Testing copies of searches** — Distinguish: titles include `_dev` suffix not in matrix. Suppress: `where match(title,"^nis2_[A-Z0-9]+$")` in rest subsearch.
• **Renamed control IDs** — Distinguish: matrix still references old `control_id`. Suppress: lookup `nis2_control_alias.csv` mapping old to new before REST join.
• **SHC captain-only REST view** — Distinguish: some members lack SplunkEnterpriseSecurity app context. Suppress: run saved search on captain with `splunk_server=local` service account role `ess_rest_reader`.
• **Intentional seasonal pause** — Distinguish: retail freezes correlation during peak sales with CISO approval. Suppress: lookup `nis2_control_pause_calendar.csv` keyed on week number.
equipmentModels: ServiceNow ITSM / Security Incident Response (SIR) module
Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x (notable framework, correlation rules)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

• **Splunk**: Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud (current). Scheduling requires a search head not under memory pressure (check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ProcessRunner` for OOM warnings before deploying a new scheduled search).
• **Lookups**: `nis2_control_matrix.csv`, `nis2_entity_classification.csv` must be deployed to the search-tier app and refreshed on a documented schedule. For KV Store lookups, verify with `| rest /servicesNS/-/-/data/transforms/lookups | search name IN ('nis2_control_matrix.csv','nis2_entity_classification.csv')`.
• **Search-head capacity**: Subsearch-based detections need `subsearch_maxtime` ≥ 60s and `subsearch_maxout` ≥ 50000 in `limits.conf`. For high-volume environments, tune `[search] max_searches_per_cpu` up from default.
• **Regulatory mapping**: This detection evidences clause `Art.21(2)`. The control objective is: "Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.32: NIS2 Proportional Security Measure Verification by Tier." — every alert payload should include this text so downstream consumers know the compliance purpose.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Data collection is bespoke for this UC — no single canonical TA parses every source the detection relies on. Route data into the relevant indexes under the relevant sourcetypes, and document the ingestion path in the UC runbook so future engineers know where each field originates.


5 operational considerations apply to the rollout of this detection:


• **Hint 1:** Prefix every mandated correlation search with `nis2_` + `control_id`. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 2:** Version `nis2_control_matrix.csv` whenever risk committee moves a control between tiers. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 3:** Save `UC-22.2.32` weekly. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 4:** Auto-assign SNOW tasks using Splunkbase 1928 alert action. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 5:** Mirror outputs into GRC workbook quarterly. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

## Step 2 — Implement the search

For "NIS2 Proportional Security Measure Verification by Tier," the aggregation produces the result shape required by the NIS2 control — each row is a summary record the regulation expects to be presentable at audit time.

This search correlates the outer dataset (index=`the outer`, sourcetype=`the primary`) with a subsearch on `entity_tier` across the full search range. `type=inner` means the search only returns rows where `entity_tier` exists in both datasets. This is the correct choice when the detection requires presence in both sources — a left-join would include orphaned rows that cannot be evaluated against the compliance rule.

Join caveat: Splunk's `| join` subsearch is capped at `max_searches_per_cpu * subsearch_maxout` results (default: 50000). If the inner subsearch exceeds this, rows are silently dropped and the resulting report will under-count. Check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd log_level=WARN subsearch` for truncation warnings. For inner searches expected to return >10000 rows, rewrite as `| stats` with a larger base search and post-filter, or use `| tstats` if a CIM datamodel covers the data.

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.2.32: NIS2 Proportional Security Measure Verification by Tier]
cron_schedule = 0 */6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = summary_compliance
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.2.32,reg=NIS2
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Field extraction check:** Run the following to confirm the TA is extracting every field the detection relies on:

```spl
index=the_index sourcetype=the_sourcetype earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| where count=0
| table field
```

Any row returned is a field the TA claims to extract but isn't appearing in recent data — it's either a rename upstream, a TA version mismatch, or a data-format change.

**(b) Required-field presence:** This detection specifically needs: `control_id`, `entity_tier`, `search_disabled`. Run:

```spl
index=the_index sourcetype=the_sourcetype earliest=-1h
| head 100
| table control_id, entity_tier, search_disabled
```

Every row should have non-null values for the fields the detection's `where` clause filters on. Nulls cause silent drops in the result set.

**(e) Negative test:** Generate a synthetic 'should not alert' event using the Splunk Sample Generator or a crafted `| makeresults` event and confirm the detection does NOT fire on it. A detection that fires on everything is equivalent to a detection that fires on nothing.

## Step 4 — Operationalize (dashboard and reporting)

Create a companion dashboard titled `UC-22.2.32: NIS2 evidence` with the following panel layout:

**Panel 1 — Matrix Heatmap Of Tier Vs Control Coverage (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 2 — Table Of Disabled Required Controls (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 3 — Single Value For Percentage Enabled (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.


Time picker defaults: set to last 30 days with named presets for 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d, and 1y — the auditor needs all five windows without retyping. Enable scheduled PDF export for the full dashboard on the regulation's reporting cadence.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

**No results when violations exist:** The most likely cause is subsearch truncation (see Step 2). Inspect `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=searchprocessor log_level=WARN subsearch` for messages like 'Output result count exceeded 50000.'

**Search times out:** Check the search job inspector (`Activity > Jobs > <this UC's last run> > Inspect`). The phase consuming the time (fetch, command, summary) indicates whether the issue is data-layer (too wide a window, unaccelerated datamodel), command-layer (expensive `transaction` or `eventstats`), or post-aggregation.


**NIS2-specific failure modes:**

• 24/7 reporting obligation (Art. 23): NIS2 requires early warning within 24 hours of awareness of a significant incident. Detections that back this SLA must compute elapsed time from the SOC's first-acknowledged timestamp (not the resolve-time) and must trigger independently of business hours — the regulatory clock does not pause overnight.

• Member-state variation: NIS2 is transposed individually by each EU Member State with some latitude on thresholds and sector scoping. A detection correct for German IT-SiG 3 (BSI) may be misaligned with Dutch CWV or French LPM. Use `nis2_member_state_config.csv` to parametrize thresholds per jurisdiction; never hardcode.

• Essential vs Important entity criteria: the applicable obligations depend on sector + size. Before alerting, verify the monitored entity's designation; a detection firing on an out-of-scope subsidiary causes unnecessary regulatory notifications that erode trust with the national CSIRT.

• Supply-chain (Art. 21(2)(d)) extent: NIS2 explicitly includes direct suppliers' cybersecurity posture. Detections must cover monitored-by-you-not-owned-by-you infrastructure (outsourced SaaS, managed SIEM tenants) — these are in scope even if operationally run by a third party. BA/BAA-style governance applies.
grandmaExplanation: We compare the "who must have which safety alarm switched on" spreadsheet to what is actually running inside the security monitoring tool, tier by tier, so nobody accidentally lives with the stricter alarms turned off—or the lighter ones left on waste mode forever.
